svg.text: SCC Algorithms — Tarjan vs Kosaraju vs Path-based
Extra arrays index, lowlink order, visited two stacks
Reversal of graph no yes (need transpose) no
Time complexity O(V + E) O(V + E) O(V + E)
Output order reverse topological topological reverse topological
After SCC, contract each SCC into a single super-vertex. The result is a DAG, the
"condensation graph" or "DAG of SCCs." Inter-SCC edges connect different super-vertices.
Tarjan emits SCCs in reverse topological order, so condensation is built incrementally.
• Reduce reachability problems on cyclic graphs to DAG problems.
• 2-SAT solving: SCC of implication graph → satisfying assignment.
SCC + condensation transforms general directed graphs into DAGs you can topo-sort.
